titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/293

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=726 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:21:22.229929+00 | translation moa=727 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:21:17.107951+00

p.cl:
Omnibus sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris litteras istas uisuris uel audituris Adam de Hetun’
et Helena uxor eius salutem in domino .

Nouerit vniuersitas uestra quod nos coram Magistro Hugone Archidiacono Glasgu
ensi . et Magistro Waltero decano de Teuidal’ et Domino Ada de bagath et multis aliis uiris discretis clericis et laicis
affidauimus et tactis sacrosanctis iurauimus quod nos nunquam contra domum de Melros de terris quas eidem domui uendidimus
in territorio de Molle querelam mouebimus . Nec alicui contra eandem domum de predictis terris querelam mouere uolenti
consenciemus .

Ad maiorem uero huius rei securitatem hoc scriptum apposicione sigillorum predicti Archidiaconi . et predicti decani .
et domini Ade de bagath’ vna cum apposictione sigilli nostri roborauimus .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future who shall view or hear these letters Adam of Heiton and Helen his wife send greeting in the Lord.

Your whole community should know that we have pledged our faith and sworn on holy relics in front of Master Hugh the archdeacon of Glasgow and Master Walter the dean of Tweeddale and the lord Adam of Bathgate and many other distinguished men, clerk and lay, that we shall never move a plaint against the monastic house of Melrose about the lands that we sold to the same house; nor shall we give willing consent to move a plaint against the same house about the aforesaid lands.

For the greater security of this matter we have verified this writing by the application of the seals of the aforesaid archdeacon and the aforesaid dean and the lord Adam of Bathgate together with the application of our own seal.
